ref: I am the Lord! There is nothing too difficult for me. I'll come back next year at the time I promised, and Sarah will already have a son.”
text: Bɛɛ n kpolle Kɔraawiizi ɛɛu huhzɛi? N feloore ŋɔ lugɔ bina saŋa naa n daŋ bir ba. N daŋ waa ba, di i haaŋ Saara lul biibaal.”
translated: Why did you leave Kɔraawiizi? I was going to the farm when I heard that. I heard that you have given birth to a baby girl.

ref: At once they saw what they had done, and they realized they were naked. Then they sewed fig leaves together to cover themselves.
text: Kakpaŋ, ba lɛɛzi chaani. Ba na lugɔ ba kpogli chakpallii. Hiihzi jaa lau baa. Awia, ba kpɛlli daa bɛl banna yir fig paŋhoori, di nyɛr tɔ ba chakpalli hiŋ.
translated: Kakpang, let's go home. We have stayed in the bush for too long. The sun is very hot. Grandmother, our food is almost finished, and we are hungry.

ref: At first, Lot just stood there. But the Lord wanted to save him. So the angels took Lot, his wife, and his two daughters by the hand and led them out of the city.
text: Lɔt faa haa ra di hɔɔzi, ka a mɛlikazi lauri u ni u haaŋ ni u biizi nonee, di tɛrgi baa lii a bolzeŋ biini, dikuubɛɛwiai, Kɔraawiizi faa suŋ baa waa ninɛɛ.
translated: The man went to the river, and he saw a beautiful woman who was bathing and washing her clothes, then he quickly hid behind a big tree, and he watched her, until the woman finished and went home.

ref: What were you thinking when you did this?
text: Ɛrɛɛ i ɛɛ yaa gunnɛi?”
translated: What did you go to do?

ref: Afterwards, Rebekah and the young women who were to travel with her prepared to leave. Then they got on camels and left with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Denii Ribɛka ni u balaa tinni ba nyugmazi, ba ni Abraham balii la maa jaa lii di ra kaali.
translated: God tested Abraham's faith by telling him to sacrifice his only son.

ref: His brothers asked, “Do you really think you are going to be king and rule over us?” Now they hated Joseph more than ever because of what he had said about his dream.
text: Denii u kpɛɛnna la ŋɔ, “I ra fɛu lugɔ hinuu niŋ ŋmɛnɛ wa ɛɛ yaa kooriaa?” Ba faa waa kaa ra nyiŋŋi u wia tama maa, dikuu u deeza hiŋ ani gunla uŋ ŋɔwɛɛ la wia.
translated: Then the chief said, "Why are you people not farming this year?" They told him that their farms were destroyed by elephants and other animals.

ref: Let's get our father drunk! Then we can sleep with him and have children.”
text: Awia, gil ya tɛu siŋ ú nyɔɔ dɛɛzi, yaa nuu chɔɔ di lul biizi.”
translated: Awia, if you do not listen to the word of your mother, you will end up suffering in the future.

ref: That's how God created the heavens and the earth. The Garden of Eden When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth,
text: Chiŋ ni hɛri tai wiarɛ keŋ. Kɔraawiizi n ta chiŋ ni hɛri la,
translated: If you don't have a farm, you are not a real person. The Koraawii people do not have farms,

ref: The Lord said to the man, “You listened to your wife and ate the fruit I told you not to eat. And so, the ground will be under a curse because of what you did. As long as you live, you will have to struggle to grow enough food.
text: U jaa ŋɔ Andama ge be, “Hiŋ sɛɛwɛ tɛɛ i haaŋ, di di a daanɔŋ la miŋ ŋɔ i gilɛɛ waa di la wia, N daŋ see hɛri nua dikuu i wia. I daŋ ɔɔ par di di tɔɔrɔɔ werge mɔɔ, ka haa na diwee di.
translated: I am Andama, and I say, "If you are a human being, then you should not have done what you did to me, causing me to suffer like this. I will also retaliate against you. You should have considered the consequences of your actions, but you did not."

ref: just as Jacob's sons were coming in from work. When they learned that their sister had been raped, they became furiously angry, because nothing is more disgraceful than rape, and it must not be tolerated.
text: Jekɔb biibambizi n wa nuu guŋ, ba lii pir di ba. Ba biine chɔgi werge, ba baana ra fuuh werge maa, dikuu gunla Sɛkɛm n pum Jekɔb biihaaŋ Daina, di du Izrɛl hina hiihzi wia, dikuubɛɛwiai, guŋ waa dɛkkɛ.
translated: Jacob's sons were ten in number, and they went to Egypt to buy grain. They did not recognize Joseph, but he recognized them, and he remembered the dreams he had dreamed about them. He then said to them, "You are spies," and he put them all in prison for three days.

ref: Laban replied, “In our country the older daughter must get married first.
text: Denii Laban ŋɔ, “Yaa naabaala wiirɛ lugɔ a waa dɛkkɛ yaa n felee kpa yaa biihambijiga tɛɛ baal, ka ka u kpɛɛma.
translated: The next day, Laban said, "Even if the chief's son is a fool, he is still my friend and a child of the family, so let him come in."

ref: and wandering through the fields, when a man asked, “What are you looking for?”
text: baal bɛl nau di u vollo ra juuri, u pɛɛzuu, “Beedee i ra nyiŋŋei?”
translated: The child asked the leopard why he was chasing him, and the leopard replied, "Don't you know me?"

ref: You were willing to offer your only son to the Lord, and so he makes you this solemn promise,
text: di ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ŋɔwɔɔrɛ, ‘N hɛɛhzi n hinnɛ lugɔ hiŋ sɛɛwɛ hiŋ n nua, di lau i biikpaŋkpaŋ í kpu, di lizi mbusu, di fuuuh tɛŋ naa wia, n na ŋɔ i benii wibii maa lugɔ
translated: And he said, "Koraawiizi said, 'I will go and bring the chief of the bush and his wife, and I will make them very small, and put them in a pot, and cook them until they are done, and then I will come back to the chief's house."

ref: Meanwhile, Jacob heard what had happened. But his sons were out in the fields with the cattle, so he did not do anything at the time.
text: Jekɔb n wa nuuwɛ lugɔ ba chɔgi u biihaaŋ Daina, di u biibaala faa haa du pir di ra binni ba sela. Awia, u faa siigi ka ra kpaŋ ba balaa.
translated: Jacob did not want his children to marry outside their tribe, so he made his children marry within the family. However, this caused problems later on.

ref: The servant explained: I am Abraham's servant.
text: A baal ŋɔ, “Abraham baliirɛ miŋ.
translated: Abraham is my father.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Awia, u ta newea, ba liu. U ta baabuu ba du u himmaa duula, di ɛɛ baal ni haaŋ.
translated: The man, he is good, he is not a thief. He has never taken anyone's property, nor has he ever killed anyone.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Awia, nyiŋŋi daananaa di kuari muugberzenzeŋ bɛl, í wer di ra zuu u biini lugɔ dia. Nyiŋŋi kotaal ham u biini ni u harbɔɔ maa nɛnnɛ.
translated: If a woman is not able to cook well, she should not be given a husband. A man should also be able to take care of his wife and children.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: N daŋ tɛi nannakpila werge, baŋhina daŋ ɛɛ koorizenaa. I daŋ dɔɔ nannakpila werge, guŋ gil bá birgi hɛrkpila hina.
translated: I went to the farm, but I did not see any animal. He also went to the farm, and he saw a big animal there.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Denii Zilpa kaa lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.
translated: Then Zilpah bore Jacob a second son.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Jekɔb n wa na baa u ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ni u mɛlikazi chɔɔllɛ keŋ!” U faa dau de sɔŋ Mahanam.
translated: When Jacob saw Rachel, the daughter of his uncle Laban, he said, "She is the one I will marry." He then went to the house of Mahana.

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: Gunnɛ Jekɔb faa ŋɔ tɛɛ a senɔɔna sugesugezi la maa di wa kaali, di karfi baa lugɔ di ba wa chem Ɛsau ni, ba maa ŋɔ wikpaŋ la uŋ ŋɔwɛɛ la tɛu.
translated: Gunnɛ Jacob told his children his last wishes and said they should bury him, and he emphasized that they should bury him in the cave that his grandfather Abraham bought from Ephron the Hittite, which is in the field of Machpelah near Mamre in the land of Canaan, where Abraham and his wife Sarah were buried, and also Isaac and his wife Rebekah were buried there, and where he buried Leah.

ref: When Esau noticed the women and children he asked, “Whose children are these?” Jacob answered, “These are the children the Lord has been kind enough to give to me, your servant.”
text: Denii Ɛsau binni di na haana la ni ba biizi la, di pɛɛzi, “Lee hinaa kennei?” Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Biizi naabuu Kɔraawiizi suŋ i balii ni, di tɛu.”
translated: When Esau heard his father's words, he cried out with a loud and bitter cry and said to him, "Bless me too, my father!" But Jacob said, "Your brother came deceitfully and took your blessing."

ref: Esau and Adah's oldest son was Eliphaz, and the clans that descended from him were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz,
text: Banniibuu faa ɛɛ koora, Ɛsau naanna biini. Ɛsau biibambilugli Ɛlifaz biibaalaa, Tɛman, Omar, Zɛfɔ, Kɛnaz,
translated: And these are the generations of Esau, who is Edom. Esau took his wives from the daughters of Canaan; Adah the daughter of Elon the Hittite, and Aholibamah the daughter of Anah the daughter of Zibeon the Hivite; And Bashemath Ishmael's daughter, sister of Nebajoth. And Adah bare to Esau Eliphaz; and Bashemath bare Reuel; And Aholibamah bare Jeush, and Jaalam, and Korah: these are the sons of Esau, which were born unto him in the land of Canaan. And Esau took his wives, and his sons, and his daughters, and all the persons of his house, and his cattle, and all his beasts, and all his substance, which he had got in the land of Canaan; and went into the country from the face of his brother Jacob. For their riches were more than that they might dwell together; and the land wherein they were strangers could not bear them because of their cattle. Thus dwelt Esau in mount Seir: Esau is Edom. And these are the generations of Esau the father of the Edomites in mount Seir: These are the names of Esau's sons; Eliphaz the son of Adah the wife of Esau, Reuel the son of Bashemath the wife of Esau. And the sons of Eliphaz were Teman, Omar, Zepho, and Gatam, and Kenaz.

ref: When Noah sobered up and learned what his youngest son had done,
text: Noowa siwee n wa ba hɛri, u nuu gunla u biibaala maa bichala Ham n ɛɛwɛ.
translated: When Noowa's wife gave birth, her husband named the child Ham and left.

ref: They also defeated the Horites in the hill country of Edom, as far as El-Paran, near the desert.

translated: The story of Hɔri and Sɛɛ is a very old story that happened in the olden days at a place called Elparan.

ref: I created humans to be like me, and I will punish any animal or person that takes a human life. If an animal kills someone, that animal must die. And if a person takes the life of another, that person must be put to death.
text: Wibii maa, nɛr la maa n kpu u dɔŋhiŋ, dɛkkɔɔ bá kpu u ge di turgi u chal kim. Di i kpu nɛr, i kpu kunla Kɔraawiizi n tawɛɛ u liubuu.
translated: The chief said that if a person kills his fellow, they should kill him too and hang him on a tree. If you kill a person, you kill the spirit of God who created him.

ref: and that he keeps cheating me by changing my wages time after time. But God has protected me.
text: ka u finimbuu di lau n kinturgaa birgi bufi. Ama, Kɔraawiizi waa sɛɛwɛ ú ɛɛŋ wichɔgii maa.
translated: I was going to the farm to harvest some corn. But, the Kɔraawiizi people came and took it by force.

ref: Then Sarai said to Abram, “It's all your fault! I gave you my slave woman, but she has been hateful to me ever since she found out she was pregnant. You have done me wrong, and you will have to answer to the Lord for this.”
text: Denii Sarai ŋɔ u jaari be, “Hinuu baani la. N yoŋhaaŋ naa kpogli hiarɛ, waa kaa ra tɔgzi, haali miŋ tɛu ŋmɛnɛɛ lugɔ i nuu ra chɔɔ gba. Awia, Kɔraawiizi daŋ turgii a kim, dikuu hiŋ ɛɛŋ guŋ wia!”
translated: Long ago, Sarai said, "I am going to visit my in-laws. When I finish sweeping the courtyard, you can pound the millet, but do not let the pestle touch the palm of your hand. If you do, you will become pregnant, and your child will be called Korawii!"

ref: Later, Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan, and when Dedan grew up, he had three sons: Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
text: Jɔksan ge faa lul biibaala banɛɛ, ba ra yir baa Siiba ani Dɛdan. Dɛdan naannaa faa Assur, Lɛtus ani Lɛyum hina maa.
translated: The first people who came to settle were Siiba and Dɛdan. Dɛdan gave birth to Assur, Lɛtus, and Lɛyum.

ref: While Abraham was still alive, he gave gifts to the sons of Hagar and Keturah. He also sent their sons to live in the east far from his son Isaac, and when Abraham died, he left everything to Isaac. The Death of Abraham
text: Ka uŋ faa haa du de ni miibɔɔ la, u faa tɛɛ u hahjigzi biibaalaa kuna, ka ta baa ŋmɛnɛɛ, ba lii u biibaal Aizik be kaali wiifurii paga hɛri.
translated: When his father saw that he was not giving him his inheritance, he became angry and went to report him to the elders, and they called his brother Isaac and gave him the entire inheritance.

ref: which showed that Joseph was his favorite son, and so Joseph's brothers hated him and would not be friendly to him.
text: U kpɛɛnna n nawɛɛ lugɔ ba mɛɛ nɔŋŋuubuu di kaali baa la, ba jaa hauh werge maa. Ba waa kaa ra ŋɔ wiŋɔnanaa di ra tɛu.
translated: When the elders of the village go to the farm, they do not go alone. They always go with their grandchildren.

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and this time the younger sister slept with him. But once again he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Too, a hamuŋa hiŋ ba kaa gil u nyɔɔ siŋ dɛɛzi, u niwee la ge zuu, u nuu chɔɔ. U ge gba n zuuwɛ u nuu chɔɔ la, uŋ wa izee di u mɛɛ waa zummɛ.
translated: The man, when he was going to the farm, his bow broke, and his hand got hurt. When he returned home because his hand was hurt, his child was not there.

ref: This rock will be your house, and I will give back to you a tenth of everything you give me.”
text: Bii naa miŋ baŋŋɛ naa ge daŋ ɛɛ bɔr la ban ninna jaamuu. Denii ń jaa kpa kuna la miŋ dɔɔwɛ fi bɔkpaŋ di tɛu.”
translated: I am the chief of this village, and I have ruled for many years. Today, I am speaking to you about the importance of unity.

ref: long years to earn your two daughters and another
text: Jekɔb faa dar Laban, waa sɛɛwɛ u zum lugɔ u ra chɔu.
translated: Jacob fled from Laban because he was afraid that he would take his wives from him.

ref: In a dream he saw a ladder that reached from earth to heaven, and God's angels were going up and down on it.
text: Unna chɔɔ dɔɔ la, u deezi di na keeraa bɛl n chigɛ hɛri di ga chug chiŋ, mɛlikazi ga u ni di ra tinni, di kaa ra tuu.
translated: When the sun was setting, he told his children to gather firewood and make a fire, and he would go and bring meat, and they would eat.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: A guŋ saŋa hiŋ, Jekɔb faa lizi balaa baŋhina, bá feli la u kpɛɛma Ɛsau be, Ɛdɔm hɛri.
translated: When Jacob saw his brother Esau coming with four hundred men, he divided his children among Leah, Rachel, and the two female servants.

ref: At this time Abram the Hebrew was living near the oaks that belonged to Mamre the Amorite. Mamre and his brothers Eshcol and Aner were Abram's friends. Someone who had escaped from the battle told Abram
text: Ka nɛra la n chɔwɛɛ lii a laal biini la bɛl chɔ di wa ŋɔ Hibru hinuu Abram be. Di Abram sogo dugli Amɔri hinuu Mamrɛ daazenaa. A Mamrɛ ni u lulla Ɛskɔl, Anɛ ani Abram faa dɔɔ nuukpannɛ.
translated: The Lord appeared to Abram and said, "I will give this land to your descendants." So Abram built an altar there to the Lord, who had appeared to him. From there he went on toward the hills east of Bethel and pitched his tent, with Bethel on the west and Ai on the east. There he built an altar to the Lord and called on the name of the Lord.

ref: The angel said, “Go back to Sarai and be her slave.
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi mɛlika la ŋɔ u be, “Bir la i dɔɔri la be di gaa sɛɛ u dɔɔra.”
translated: The King of Denii Koraawiizi said, "If a person does something, it will be done to them."

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:

translated: When Jacob had finished praying, he looked up and saw Esau coming with his four hundred men.

ref: If the woman refuses to come along, you don't have to keep this promise. But don't ever take my son back there.”
text: Di a biihambie waa wa sɛɛwɛ lugɔ u hiŋii ba, guŋwaa, i lii a hɛɛhzaa biinii. Ama, a dazi i ŋmɛnɛ maa wa kpogli n biibaal naa di ra la de.”
translated: The chief told his people that he was going to travel to a faraway land and that they should take good care of the village in his absence. However, he warned them that if he returned and found any problems, he would hold them responsible.
